title: 10 Herbal Creams For Chickenpox
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan if you or your child has chickenpox. These creams often contain soothing herbs like Calendula officinalis, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply a Calendula cream to chickenpox blisters, it can help reduce redness and swelling, making the skin feel more comfortable.  Herbal teas, on the other hand, can help relieve chickenpox symptoms from the inside out. For example, Echinacea purpurea is an herb that is often used to boost the immune system and fight off infections. Drinking Echinacea tea can help your body fight off the chickenpox virus more effectively, reducing the severity and duration of the illness.  Another herb that can be beneficial for chickenpox is Aloe barbadensis, which is often used in creams and ointments to soothe and calm irritated skin. When you apply an Aloe cream to chickenpox blisters, it can help reduce itching and inflammation, making the skin feel cooler and more comfortable.  Using herbal creams and teas for chickenpox can be a natural and gentle way to relieve symptoms and support your body's recovery. By incorporating these herbs into your treatment plan, you can help reduce the discomfort and duration of chickenpox, and get back to your normal activities sooner.  Some benefits of using herbal creams and teas for chickenpox include reduced itching and inflammation, which can make it easier to sleep and go about your daily activities. You may also experience fewer complications, such as scarring or skin infections, which can occur when chickenpox blisters become infected.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains flavonoids, triterpene saponins, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin affected by chickenpox. The flavonoids in Calendula officinalis creams can reduce redness and swelling, while the triterpene saponins can help to prevent bacterial infections that can occur with chickenpox. The carotenoids in Calendula officinalis creams can also promote wound healing and skin regeneration, which can help to reduce the risk of scarring. By reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, Calendula officinalis creams can help to alleviate symptoms of chickenpox and support the healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Calendula officinalis flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool before use. Apply 2-3 times a day to soothe chickenpox skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides and glycosides, including cichoric acid and caftaric acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and redness associated with chickenpox. The alkaloids in Echinacea purpurea, such as echinacoside and isobutylamides, also have immune-boosting properties that can help the body fight off the virus that causes chickenpox. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent bacterial infections that can develop as a result of scratching the skin. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams to the affected areas, it can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Echinacea flowers in a low oven (150°F) for 2 hours or until they are completely dry.
In a double boiler, melt the coconut oil and beeswax together. Stir until smooth.
Add the dried Echinacea flowers and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool. Store in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains several bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, which help soothe and calm the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox. Aloe-emodin has antiviral properties that may help combat viral infections like chickenpox, while acemannan promotes wound healing and tissue repair. The soothing and moisturizing effects of aloin and other compounds in aloe barbadensis creams help to reduce itching and promote the healing of blisters and sores. The antimicrobial properties of aloe-emodin also help prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for skin healing.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of tea tree oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 10-15 seconds.
Add the Aloe barbadensis gel and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Add the tea tree oil and stir well. Let the mixture cool and thicken for 10-15 minutes.
Once thickened, pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains the active compound cineole, along with terpinen-4-ol, which gives it potent antiviral and antibacterial properties. These properties help to reduce the severity of symptoms associated with chickenpox, such as fever and skin infections. The tea tree oil in Melaleuca alternifolia creams has been shown to exhibit antipruritic properties, which can help to soothe itchy skin and reduce discomfort. Additionally, its antiseptic properties can help to prevent secondary infections, such as bacterial infections, that can occur when the skin is broken due to chickenpox. When applied topically, the compounds in Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help to promote healing and reduce the risk of complications associated with chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 tablespoons of honey, 10 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Remove melted mixture from heat and add olive oil, honey, and Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil. Stir well.
Pour mixture into small containers or a tin lined with parchment paper. Let it cool and harden.
Once hardened, label and store the creams in a cool, dry place. Apply a small amount to affected skin as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two active constituents known for their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties help to reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms, such as redness and swelling. The antiseptic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate in Lavandula angustifolia creams also help to prevent infection and promote healing of the skin. Additionally, the soothing effects of these constituents can help to calm and comfort itchy, irritated skin. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Lavandula angustifolia creams can help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until it's fully dissolved.
Add the dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the mixture and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, then let it cool and solidify before applying to affected skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains bioactive constituents like hyperforin and hypericin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the redness and swelling associated with chickenpox. Hyperforin also has antiviral properties, which may inhibit the spread of the virus that causes chickenpox, allowing the body to recover faster. Additionally, the creams contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm the skin. By applying Hypericum perforatum creams, the skin may benefit from the anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects of its bioactive constituents.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1/2 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Let the mixture cool and thicken, then store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains the active constituents eucalyptol and cineole, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox, making it easier to manage the condition. Cineole also has a cooling effect, which can provide relief from the itching and discomfort caused by chickenpox sores. The antiseptic properties of eucalyptol help prevent infections from developing in the skin, reducing the risk of complications. By applying eucalyptus globulus creams to the affected areas, you can help soothe and calm the skin, promoting faster healing.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, 10 drops of eucalyptus oil, and 5 drops of tea tree oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted and smooth.
Add shea butter and stir until melted and well combined. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool and thicken. Add vitamin E oil, eucalyptus oil, and tea tree oil. Mix well.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool completely. Once set, your eucalyptus globulus cream is ready to use on chickenpox lesions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major creams contains a compound called aucubin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the swelling and redness associated with chickenpox. Another compound, baicalein, found in the cream, has antiviral properties that can help combat the virus that causes chickenpox. The cream also contains aucubin's cousin, 6-hydroxyaucubin, which has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these compounds can help soothe and calm the skin, making it easier to manage the discomfort and pain caused by chickenpox. Plantago major creams also contains other compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids that have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent secondary infections.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago major herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Plantago major leaves, 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Chop the Plantago major leaves into small pieces and mix with aloe vera gel in a bowl.
In a separate bowl, melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments.
Combine the melted oil mixture with the Plantago major and aloe vera mixture, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool and harden. Use as needed to soothe chickenpox skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains a key compound called allantoin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. It also contains mucilages like glucomannan, which help soothe irritated skin and reduce itching. The cream's soothing properties can calm the skin and help reduce the discomfort associated with chickenpox. Symphytum officinale also contains astringent tannins that can help reduce the severity of the rash. By applying Symphytum officinale creams to affected areas, you can help alleviate some of the symptoms of chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Symphytum officinale flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Symphytum officinale flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours. Let them cool completely.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler. Stir until smooth.
Add the dried Symphytum officinale flowers to the melted mixture and stir well. Let it infuse for 2 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth.
Add the vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the cream into a container and let it cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contains active constituents like histamine, acetylcholine, and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties help reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms, such as redness and swelling. The saponins in Urtica dioica creams also have an antiviral effect, which may help prevent the spread of the virus. Histamine and acetylcholine in the cream help reduce itching and promote skin healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing, Urtica dioica creams may help alleviate some of the discomfort associated with chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Clean the Urtica dioica leaves and dry them with a towel. Grind them into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat. Add the beeswax and stir until melted.
Add the Urtica dioica paste to the melted oil mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or container and let it cool and solidify. Use the cream as needed to soothe chickenpox rashes.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help boost your immune system, but it won't prevent chickenpox from forming. Chickenpox is a contagious illness caused by a virus, and herbal tea can't fight off the virus. However, staying hydrated and eating well can help your body recover faster if you do get chickenpox.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be soothing for chickenpox, it's not recommended to drink them daily. Some herbs can interact with your body's healing process, slowing it down. It's best to limit herbal tea consumption to when you really need it, like when you're feeling stressed or uncomfortable.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with chickenpox symptoms, but it's not a cure. They might take a few days to a week to show results, depending on the tea and individual body. Some teas, like calendula and peppermint, may soothe skin and reduce fever faster than others. Results can vary from person to person.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for chickenpox can be most helpful in the evening. This is because many herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have calming properties that can help soothe an itchy, uncomfortable body. Sipping on a warm cup before bed can promote a restful night's sleep, which is especially important when you're not feeling well.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with certain prescription medications for chickenpox, such as antiviral drugs. This is because some herbs, like St. John's Wort, can affect how these medications work in your body. If you're taking prescription meds for chickenpox and drinking herbal teas, it's possible that the herbs could change the way the medications are absorbed or processed.
faq_pregnant: It's best to avoid herbal teas while pregnant with chickenpox. Some herbs, like echinacea and calendula, may stimulate the immune system, which isn't recommended during pregnancy. Additionally, herbal teas can interact with medications or have unknown effects on the fetus. Stick to bland fluids like water and clear broth to stay hydrated.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for chickenpox are not recommended while breastfeeding. Some herbs may pass into breast milk and affect the baby. If you're breastfeeding, it's best to stick to plain water or other safe teas. Chickenpox is usually treated with rest, hydration, and antiviral medication, so it's best to follow those recommendations instead of relying on herbal remedies.
title_2: Chickenpox Solutions for Nurses: 10 Herbal Creams to Try
